description: RSS / Atom feeds SCOUT polls every 4 hours. Each entry: vendor slug (matches Supabase tools.slug where applicable), feed URL, type, categorization hints, and a tracked flag SCOUT uses to decide whether to poll. URLs marked tracked=false either failed verification or never had RSS to begin with (see dead_as_of / dead_reason). RSS is a SUPPLEMENTARY signal in the 2026 stack — see _notes for the primary/supplementary/roadmap split established after the 2026-05-22 audit.
verification_protocol: 1. curl -IL (follow redirects) and check final 200 + content-type contains 'xml' or 'rss' — catches dead-on-arrival URLs but misses content-type lies.
2. curl -L to fetch the body — confirm it starts with '<?xml' and contains <item> or <entry> elements. Yotpo-style content-type lies (header says rss+xml, body is HTML homepage) only get caught here.
3. Count <item>/<entry> > 0 AND most-recent <pubDate>/<updated> within last 90 days — catches valid-skeleton-but-empty feeds (nofraud-style) and abandoned blogs (cogsy, last post June 2023).
4. Only if all three pass: flip tracked=true.

5. If 404 or empty body: probe common alternative paths via curl loop with a real browser User-Agent: /feed/, /feed, /rss, /rss/, /blog/feed/, /blog/rss.xml, /blog/atom.xml, /blog/feed.xml, /feed.xml, /atom.xml, /news/feed, /resources/feed, /changelog/feed.xml. Many vendors hide the URL one click deeper than the blog index.

6. If absolutely no working RSS path exists after path-probing: mark tracked=false + dead_as_of=YYYY-MM-DD + dead_reason='<specific reason>'. Add vendor to the newsletter-ingestion roadmap (see _notes) — for most 2026 marketing sites that have no public RSS, email is the working substitute.

feeds.notes: Shopify developer changelog (1852 items as of audit) — captures platform changes, Editions, Sidekick/Magic releases directly at the source. Swapped 2026-05-22 from dead https://www.shopify.com/blog.atom (returned HTML, not Atom). This feed is a stricter upgrade for SCOUT's purpose: less marketing noise, more product-truth.

feeds.dead_reason: Feed parses, but last post is 2023-12-07 — Shopify abandoned this press-release feed. Considered as fallback for shopify-changelog but rejected as stale. Kept as audit tombstone.
feeds.notes: Press-release feed — historically used for corporate announcements but unmaintained since end-2023. Re-check in 6 months.

feeds.dead_reason: URL returns explicit 404 page (React-Router fallback served with status 200, but body is Klaviyo's 'Page not found' template). No working RSS path discovered after probing /feed, /rss, /blog/feed, /blog/rss, /blog/rss.xml, /blog/atom.xml, /news.atom, help.klaviyo.com/hc/en-us/articles.rss. Klaviyo's blog runs on a marketing-site stack without RSS exposure.
feeds.notes: CRITICAL coverage gap — pricing announcements + Customer Agent / Marketing Agent feature drops land here first. Must be covered via newsletter ingestion (roadmap) + pricing-page scrape (primary).

URL updated 2026-05-22 from /blog/feed (404) to /blog/rss.xml. Verified: 1 item visible at audit time, latest 22 May 2026 — low cadence.

_notes: Total entries: 40 (39 original + shopify-news tombstone added 2026-05-22). Active tracked: 17. Verified dead (no usable RSS): 20. Stale (RSS exists but abandoned or empty): 3.
Full verification performed 2026-05-22 — see /sessions/infra-log.md '2026-05-22 (session 2) — RSS pivot' block for findings, methodology, and architectural implications.

PRIMARY: pricing-page scrape (Playwright, weekly Sunday 04:00 UTC) + Reddit/community monitoring (daily 10:00 UTC). These catch changes at the source — pricing-page diffs see vendor changes the day they ship; Reddit catches operator chatter within hours of any vendor move. RSS lags both.

SUPPLEMENTARY: RSS feeds (17 working entries above). Free signal where available, but do not over-weight in CHIEF briefings — empirical coverage is only ~45% of target vendors, and even working feeds often run on marketing-blog cadence (latency vs the real change).

ROADMAP: newsletter ingestion via a dedicated Beehiiv inbox covers the 20 verified-dead vendors (klaviyo, postscript, loop, triple-whale, attentive, mailchimp, manychat, yotpo, judge-me, limespot, nosto, northbeam, polar-analytics, adcreative-ai, redo, hypotenuse-ai, pebblely, photoroom, pencil, emarketer). Email is where modern SaaS marketing actually publishes — high deliverability, vendor-maintained. Setup estimate: 1-2 days (dedicated mailbox e.g. news@botapolis.com → Beehiiv inbox API hookup → SCOUT email parser writing to content_opportunities). HIGH-PRIORITY follow-up — single biggest signal-coverage gain available.

Why so many dead RSS: 2026 marketing-site stack (Webflow / Framer / Next.js / HubSpot CMS / custom React) does not expose RSS by default. This is a structural shift, not a config bug. Periodic re-check (~6 months) for vendors that might add RSS later — particularly nofraud (empty skeleton today) and cogsy (abandoned 2023).
Re-tested URLs are still subject to vendor-side changes — SCOUT logs every fetch result to agent_logs; weekly audit catches drift. The verification_protocol above is intended for re-runs.
